# User Story: 02 - Test DLP Inspection for Plugin API Traffic

**As a** Security Architect,
**I want** to validate that our DLP solution (Zscaler) can actually inspect and enforce policies on API traffic generated by the Outlook plugin,
**so that** we can confirm that sensitive documents cannot be exfiltrated through plugin-based channels (reducing the critical control gap).

## Acceptance Criteria

* A DLP test plan is created with realistic attack scenarios (e.g., email containing M&A terms, confidential deal data)
* Real test cases are executed using the plugin to transmit content with sensitive keywords/patterns
* Results document whether Zscaler can intercept plugin API calls and detect policy violations
* Gap assessment identifies if plugin traffic bypasses DLP inspection (due to encryption, API structure, etc.)
* Findings are documented with recommended mitigations (e.g., additional inspection rules, network re-architecture)
* DLP test results are signed off before production deployment

## Notes

* This is **currently a critical unknown** — earlier discussion confirmed DLP may not cover plugin-generated API traffic
* Web uploads can be inspected, but plugin API behavior is uncertain
* If DLP cannot inspect plugin traffic, this becomes a mandatory control gap requiring alternative safeguards
* Essential prerequisite for risk acceptance sign-off
